decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since the heat spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500.0), we cannot increase it further. The next most effective lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve vertical heat extraction towards the cooled boundaries, adhering to the 2.0x step ratio constraint.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity improves the thermal path from the chip through the base to the side sinks, helping to reduce the peak temperature when the spreader is already maxed out.

expected_effects: Improved overall thermal gradient across the base plate
risk_notes: Increasing base conductivity may have diminishing returns if the bottleneck remains lateral spreading in the upper layers.
Future iterations may require geometric adjustments (spreader width/height) if material tuning saturates.
